---
title: "CV"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Education

+ PhD Psychology: *The University of Edinburgh* (2012 -- present)
+ MSc Human Cognitive Neuropsychology: *The University of Edinburgh* (2011 -- 2012)
+ BSc (hons) Psychology: *The University of Leeds* (2008 -- 2011)

## Publications

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., & Logie, R. H. (2015). Ageing and Feature Binding in Visual Working Memory: The Role of Presentation Time. *The Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, online ahead of print. [[link]](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/17470218.2015.1038571)

Brown, L. A., Niven, E. H., Logie, R. H., Rhodes, S., & Allen, R. J. (2016). Visual Feature Binding in Younger and Older Adults: Encoding and Suffix Interference Effects. *Memory*, in press.

Rhodes, S., & Parra, M. A. (in press). Executive Functioning. Encyclopedia of Geropsychology, Springer.

## Forthcoming

Parra, M. A., Ibanez, A., Rhodes, S., Baglivo, F., Kargieman, L., Garcia, M. C., Silva, W., Ciraolo, C., Vaucheret, E., Sigman, M., Bekinschtein, T. A., Starr, J., Manes, F., & Della Sala, S. (under review). Behavioural and Electrophysiological Correlates of Visual Working Memory Capacity During Change Detection Tasks. Submitted to Psychological Methods.

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (in prep). Healthy Ageing and Visual Working Memory: The Role of Mixing Feature and Conjunction Changes.

## Selected Posters and Presentations

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2015, Nov). Visual Work- ing Memory and Change Detection: Reassessing Whole Display Interference. Poster presented at the annual meeting of the Psychonomic Society, Chicago, Illinois.

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Nov). The Effect of Age on Visual Working Memory for Features and Conjunctions. Poster presented at the annual meeting of the Psychonomic Society, Long Beach, California.

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Sep). Does Healthy Ageing Affect the Ability to Bind Features in Visual Working Memory? Presented at the Seventh European Working Memory Symposium, Edinburgh.

Rhodes, S., Parra, M. A., Cowan, N., & Logie, R. H. (2014, Apr). Is Feature Binding in Visual Working Memory Age Invariant? Poster presented at the Cognitive Aging Conference 2014, Atlanta, Georgia.
